scenarios.description: cloning an empty Document produces a distinct Document with same baseUri, location, quirksMode, no children, and independent outputSettings and parser instances
scenarios.precondition: Document orig = new Document("http://example.com"); // no children, default settings
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Document is not the same instance as orig; .baseUri() equals "http://example.com"; .location() equals "http://example.com"; .quirksMode() equals QuirksMode.noQuirks; .childNodeSize() == 0; returned.outputSettings() != orig.outputSettings() but .equals(orig.outputSettings()); returned.parser() != orig.parser() but .getName().equals(orig.parser().getName()); orig and returned have independent mutable state

scenarios.description: cloning a Document with attributes and children preserves attribute values and clones children independently
scenarios.expectedResult: copy.attr("data-test") equals "value"; copy.body().selectFirst("p").text().equals("text"); modifications to orig attributes or children do not affect copy
pseudocode: TEST TC02 clone with attributes and children

scenarios.description: cloning preserves custom quirksMode and then original change of quirksMode does not affect clone
scenarios.expectedResult: copy.quirksMode() == QuirksMode.limitedQuirks; if orig.quirksMode set to noQuirks after clone, copy remains limitedQuirks
pseudocode: TEST TC03 clone preserves quirksMode immutably
